lines.en_lit: is there any matter that we are unable to perform and our agents are able to perform? This is what they say to him: We administer an oath to you according to our understanding and the understanding of the court,

he would leave and cry and they would leave and cry. He turned aside and cried that they suspected him a Sadducee; and they turned aside and cried, as Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi said: One who suspects the innocent is afflicted in his body.
And why so he would not prepare outside and bring into in the manner the Sadducees did.

The Sages taught an incident involving a Sadducee who prepared the incense outside brought Upon his emergence he was overjoyed The father of met him and said to him: My son, although we are Sadducees we fear the Pharisees said to his All my days I have been troubled over this verse: “For I will appear in the cloud above the Ark cover” I said: When will become available to me, and I will fulfill it Now that has become available to me, I not fulfill it?

said: Not a few days until he died and was laid out in the garbage and worms were coming out of his nose And some say he was struck as he emerged as Rabbi Ḥiyya taught: A type of sound was heard in the courtyard, as an angel came and struck him in the face. And his fellow priests came in and they found the likeness of a footprint of a calf between his shoulders. as it is stated “And their feet were straight feet, and the sole of their feet was like the sole of a calf’s foot”
Rabbi Zekharya ben Kevutal Rav Ḥanan bar Rava taught this to Ḥiyya bar Rav before Rav Rabbi Zekharya bar Kefutal said, and Rav demonstrated with his hand Kevutal. Let him say it. was reciting Shema

And is of that sort, permitted Didn’t Rabbi Yitzḥak bar Shmuel bar Marta say: One who is reciting Shema should neither make allusions with his eyes, nor open and close with his lips nor gesture with his fingers? And it was taught Rabbi Elazar Ḥisma says: Concerning one who recites Shema and makes allusions with his eyes, or opens and closes with his lips, or gestures with his fingers, the verse says: “And you did not call out to Me, O Jacob”
not difficult. This in first paragraph that in second paragraph
the Sages taught: and you shall talk of them of them, but not of prayer, And you shall talk of them it is permitted to speak these matters other matters

Rabbi Aḥa says: Talk of them render them, a permanent and not render them a temporary Rava said: One who engages in idle chatter violates positive as it is stated: And you shall talk of them; of them and not of other matters. Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov said: one violates a negative as it is stated: “All these matters are wearisome; no man can ever state them”

MISHNA: sought to sleep the young priests snap the middle [tzerada] finger before him, and they say to him My Master, High Priest. Stand and chill once on the floor And they engage him until the time would arrive to slaughter the
GEMARA: What tzerada Rav Yehuda said: It is the rival [tzara] of that [da] is it? the thumb; Rav Huna demonstrated the sound traveled throughout Rav’s study hall.
they said to him: My Master, High Priest. chill once on the floor Rav Yitzḥak said Introduce something new. What is it They say to him: Demonstrate bowing kidda].

And they engage him until the time to slaughter the would arrive. taught: They would not occupy him with a harp or a lyre, but with mouths. And what would they say? “Unless the Lord builds the house, its builders labor in vain on it;

the prominent of Jerusalem would not sleep the entire night so that High Priest would hear sound of noise and sleep would not overcome him It was taught Abba Shaul said: They would do so even in the outlying areas in acknowledgment of the Temple; however, that they would sin,

Abaye said, and some say Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak Interpret Neharde’a, as Elijah said to Rav Yehuda, brother Rav Salla Ḥasida: You have said Why has the Messiah not come? Isn’t today Yom Kippur, and relations were had with several virgins in Neharde’a, said to him: What did the Holy One, Blessed be He, say He said:
